tasks.title: Extend PipelineState type and startPipeline store action to capture runId
tasks.description: Add optional `runId?: string` field to the `PipelineState` interface in `frontend/src/types/index.ts`. In `useAppStore.ts`, after the `POST /api/v1/runs` call (inside `startPipeline` and `executeOrchestratorRun`), store the returned `run.runId` in `pipelineState.runId`. This is the source of truth for the log panel.
acceptance_criteria: `PipelineState` interface has `runId?: string` field.
After `startPipeline` completes, `useAppStore.getState().pipelineState.runId` is a non-empty string equal to the runId returned by `POST /api/v1/runs`.
Existing tests for `startPipeline` and `executeOrchestratorRun` still pass.
TypeScript compiles without errors (`tsc --noEmit`).

tasks.title: Create usePipelineLogStore Zustand store
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/stores/usePipelineLogStore.ts`. State: `logPanelOpen: boolean`, `selectedStageIndex: number`, `stageLogs: Record<number, string>`, `stageLoading: Record<number, boolean>`, `stageErrors: Record<number, string | null>`. Actions: `setLogPanelOpen`, `setSelectedStageIndex`, `setStageLog`, `setStageLoading`, `setStageError`, `clearStageLogs`. Export a typed selector hook `usePipelineLogStore`.
acceptance_criteria: Store file exists at `frontend/src/stores/usePipelineLogStore.ts`.
All state fields initialize to their defaults (open=false, selectedStageIndex=0, stageLogs={}, etc.).
`setStageLog(2, 'hello')` sets `stageLogs[2] === 'hello'`.
`clearStageLogs()` resets `stageLogs`, `stageLoading`, `stageErrors` to empty objects.
Unit test file `frontend/__tests__/stores/usePipelineLogStore.test.ts` exists and all assertions pass.

tasks.title: Add getStageLog function to api/client.ts
tasks.description: Add `getStageLog(runId: string, stageIndex: number, tail = 500): Promise<string>` to `frontend/src/api/client.ts`. Fetches `GET /api/v1/runs/:runId/stages/:stageIndex/log?tail=:tail`. Returns the raw text body on 200. Throws a typed error with `{ code: string, status: number }` on 4xx/5xx. On 404 with code `LOG_NOT_AVAILABLE`, throw a specific `LogNotAvailableError` so callers can distinguish 'not started yet' from 'real error'.
acceptance_criteria: `api.getStageLog('runId', 0, 500)` performs a GET to `/api/v1/runs/runId/stages/0/log?tail=500`.
Returns the response body as string on 200.
Throws `LogNotAvailableError` when server returns 404 with `{ error: { code: 'LOG_NOT_AVAILABLE' } }`.
Throws a generic error on other 4xx/5xx.
Unit test covers both happy path and `LogNotAvailableError` case.

tasks.title: Create usePipelineLogPolling hook
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/hooks/usePipelineLogPolling.ts`. Accepts `{ runId: string | null, stageIndex: number, isRunActive: boolean }`. When `runId` is non-null and panel is open: fetch immediately on mount and on `stageIndex` change, then poll every 2000 ms only if `isRunActive === true`. On `LogNotAvailableError`: set no error, leave log empty (caller renders 'not started yet'). On other error: call `setStageError(stageIndex, message)`. Clean up interval on unmount or when inputs change.
acceptance_criteria: Hook is exported from `frontend/src/hooks/usePipelineLogPolling.ts`.
When `isRunActive=false`, a single fetch is made at mount; no interval is set.
When `isRunActive=true`, fetches repeat every 2000 ms.
Interval is cleared on unmount (no memory leaks â€” verified via React Testing Library `act` + fake timers).
On `LogNotAvailableError`: `stageErrors[stageIndex]` remains null; `stageLogs[stageIndex]` is empty string.
On HTTP 500: `stageErrors[stageIndex]` is set to an error message.
Unit test covers: initial fetch, polling interval, LogNotAvailableError path, generic error path, cleanup.

tasks.title: Create LogViewer component
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/components/pipeline-log/LogViewer.tsx`. Renders `<pre ref={containerRef}>` with the log content. Auto-scrolls to bottom on each content update if the user has not manually scrolled up (`isAtBottom` state tracked via `onScroll`). Shows 'Scroll to bottom' button when not at bottom. Empty state: 'Stage not started yet.' when content is empty + `isPending`, spinner + 'Waiting for output...' when content is empty + `isRunning`. Accepts props: `content: string`, `isPending: boolean`, `isRunning: boolean`, `isLoading: boolean`, `error: string | null`.
acceptance_criteria: Component renders log content in a `<pre>` element with Tailwind `font-mono text-xs` classes.
Auto-scroll fires when `content` changes and `isAtBottom` is true.
Auto-scroll does NOT fire when user has scrolled up (isAtBottom=false).
'Scroll to bottom' button appears when not at bottom and clicking it scrolls to bottom.
Empty state shows 'Stage not started yet.' when `isPending=true` and content is empty.
Spinner + message when `isRunning=true` and content is empty.
Error string shown in error color when `error !== null`.
Unit test covers: auto-scroll on content change, scroll-up disables auto-scroll, empty state variants.

tasks.title: Create StageTabBar component
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/components/pipeline-log/StageTabBar.tsx`. Renders one tab per stage in the run. Each tab shows: short label (Architect/UX/Dev/QA, or first word of agentId), status icon (check for completed, spinning progress_activity for running, close for failed, hourglass_empty for pending). Active tab has `bg-primary/10 text-primary border-b-2 border-primary`. Props: `stages: string[]`, `stageStatuses: StageStatus[]`, `selectedIndex: number`, `onSelect: (index: number) => void`. `StageStatus` is the shape from `GET /api/v1/runs/:runId` response.
acceptance_criteria: Renders one button per stage.
Active tab has `border-b-2 border-primary` class.
Status icon is `check` for completed, rotating `progress_activity` for running, `close` for failed, `hourglass_empty` for pending.
Clicking a tab calls `onSelect(index)`.
Unit test: renders correct number of tabs, active tab styling, onSelect callback.

tasks.title: Create PipelineLogPanel container component
tasks.description: Create `frontend/src/components/pipeline-log/PipelineLogPanel.tsx`. Reads `selectedStageIndex`, `stageLogs`, `stageLoading`, `stageErrors` from `usePipelineLogStore`. Reads `pipelineState` (for `runId`, `stages`, `stageStatuses`, `status`) from `useAppStore`. Mounts `usePipelineLogPolling` with the active runId and selectedStageIndex. Renders: resizable aside using `usePanelResize({ storageKey: 'prism:panel-width:pipeline-log', defaultWidth: 480, minWidth: 320, maxWidth: 900 })`, panel header with title 'Pipeline Logs' and close button, `<StageTabBar>`, `<LogViewer>`.
acceptance_criteria: Panel renders as an `<aside>` with `border-l border-border bg-surface` Tailwind classes.
Panel width is 480px by default and resizable.
Close button (Ă—) calls `setLogPanelOpen(false)`.
Stage tabs match the stages from `pipelineState.stages`.
Log content for the selected stage is displayed in `<LogViewer>`.
Polling is active when `pipelineState.status === 'running'`.
Component test: renders panel, close button works, stage selection updates LogViewer content.

tasks.title: Add PipelineLogToggle button to Header
tasks.description: Modify `frontend/src/components/layout/Header.tsx` to add a `PipelineLogToggle` button (inline, no new file needed). The button uses Material Symbol icon `article`. It is visible only when `pipelineState !== null`. Clicking it toggles `logPanelOpen` in `usePipelineLogStore`. The button should follow the same styling as other header icon buttons (e.g., TerminalToggle).
acceptance_criteria: Button is not rendered when `pipelineState === null`.
Button is rendered when `pipelineState !== null`.
Clicking toggles `usePipelineLogStore.logPanelOpen`.
Icon is `article` from Material Symbols.
Unit test: button hidden when no pipeline, visible when pipeline active, click toggles store.

tasks.title: Mount PipelineLogPanel in App.tsx
tasks.description: Modify `frontend/src/App.tsx` to import and conditionally render `<PipelineLogPanel />` inside the flex panel row (alongside TerminalPanel, RunHistoryPanel, ConfigPanel). Condition: `logPanelOpen && pipelineState !== null`.
acceptance_criteria: `<PipelineLogPanel />` is rendered inside the `flex flex-1 overflow-hidden` div.
It renders only when `logPanelOpen === true` (from `usePipelineLogStore`).
No regressions in other panels (TerminalPanel, RunHistoryPanel, ConfigPanel still render correctly).
Integration test: PipelineLogPanel appears in DOM when logPanelOpen=true and pipelineState is set.

tasks.title: Write unit and integration tests for the log viewer feature
tasks.description: Ensure test coverage >90% for all new files: `usePipelineLogStore`, `usePipelineLogPolling`, `LogViewer`, `StageTabBar`, `PipelineLogPanel`. Tests live in `frontend/__tests__/`. Use Vitest + React Testing Library + fake timers for polling tests. Mock `api.getStageLog` at the module level.
acceptance_criteria: `npm test` inside `frontend/` passes with 0 failures.
Coverage for all new files reported by Vitest is â‰Ą 90%.
Polling hook tests use fake timers to avoid real 2s waits.
Auto-scroll behaviour is tested via `scrollTop` / `scrollHeight` spy.
No snapshot tests â€” only behaviour-based assertions.
